payload.body: CodeGraph is ready for cross-chat testing. Implemented launch-anywhere bootstrap with codegraph init [path], self-bootstrapping codegraph mcp [path], Claude hooks, AGENTS.md for Codex guidance, MCP graph auto-refresh via SQLite data_version, incremental index skipping unchanged files, repository doctor checks, bench index, and test-acceptance covering design_spec section 13. Important caveat: durable memory is currently written to .codegraph/ops, but .codegraph/ is gitignored, so this memory will not survive push/clone unless op logs/config are explicitly tracked or sync/handoff is implemented.
payload.title: Step 11 acceptance-ready state

payload.body: Smoke test handoff written through the rebuilt CodeGraph MCP server. Context: validating the new write_handoff tool after rebuild/restart.
payload.title: Codex handoff smoke test

payload.body: Reviewed HANDOFF.md and current working tree on 2026-06-22. HANDOFF.md documents completed milestones through Step 11: doctor, bench index, and acceptance tests, with next milestone listed as post-acceptance polish. Current uncommitted work extends that baseline: src/cpp_frontend.cpp now recurses through anonymous namespace/class-like scopes without emitting a scope symbol, and src/main.cpp adds a test fixture proving functions inside anonymous namespaces are indexed. Handoff MCP work is in progress: src/memory_reads.* now models ResumeContext with found, affected_nodes, unresolved_affects, and hydrated AffectedNodeView data; src/mcp_server.cpp returns the actual affected node JSON from resume_from_handoff instead of an empty vector. src/mcp_server.h no longer owns the old reduced AffectedNodeView. MCP launch/debug work is also present: .mcp.json now uses VS Code style servers config launching /bin/bash with codegraph-mcp.sh, and codegraph-mcp.sh logs wrapper context before execing build-ninja/codegraph mcp /home/yash/codegraph. Verification performed after review: cmake --build build-ninja reported no work to do; ./build-ninja/codegraph test-mcp passed; ./build-ninja/codegraph test-memory passed; ./build-ninja/codegraph test-index passed when rerun sequentially; ./build-ninja/codegraph test-acceptance passed when rerun sequentially. A parallel test attempt caused transient shared-test-db failures, not reproduced sequentially. Known issue in live repo DB: ./build-ninja/codegraph doctor /home/yash/codegraph currently fails with nodes_bad_kind: 1 because the live graph contains a handoff node; the isolated acceptance doctor still passes. Next agent should update doctor vocabulary/checks to accept handoff nodes, consider adding resume_from_handoff coverage to test-mcp, and decide whether codegraph-mcp.sh plus .mcp.json should be committed or treated as local VS Code troubleshooting.
payload.title: Post-Step 11 working-tree handoff
